channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: LeBron James has 'not much concern' after exiting loss to Celtics with late groin injury
item.description: LeBron James didn’t sound overly concerned about the strained groin that he suffered in the waning minutes of Los Angeles’ loss to Boston.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Hightower, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Is winning the F1 title in 2025 a trap? A looming rules reset could punish this year's contenders
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Could winning the Formula 1 title in 2025 actually hurt a team? That's the dilemma facing F1's team principals as sweeping regulation changes for 2026 threaten to upend the competitive order.
item.dc:creator: JAMES ELLINGWORTH, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Tatum scores 40, Celtics beat Lakers 111-101 after James leaves late with groin injury
item.description: Jayson Tatum had 40 points, 12 rebounds, and eight assists, and the Boston Celtics held off a late rally to beat the Los Angeles Lakers 111-101.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Hightower, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: No. 2 USC, No. 4 UCLA ready to square off in Round 3 with another Big Ten championship on the line
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — UCLA center Lauren Betts and her teammates used a five-day break to dissect their flaws, make a few fixes and recommit themselves to beating No. 2 Southern California — if they earned that chance at the Big Ten Tournament. Now it's all set.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL MAROT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Chiefs WR Xavier Worthy released after prosecutor declines charges
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Xavier Worthy was released Saturday after a district attorney in Texas declined to pursue charges against the Kansas City Chiefs wide receiver following an arrest in which he had been accused of felony domestic violence.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO and DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Collin Morikawa birdies final hole for a 1-shot lead at Bay Hill
item.description: ORLANDO, Fla. (AP) — Collin Morikawa left Bay Hill a year ago with an 80 on his scorecard and a second straight missed cut in the Arnold Palmer Invitational. That wasn't on his mind when he arrived to this signature event because it was more about the state of his game than the golf course.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chiefs' Xavier Worthy avoids charges from arrest, district attorney says
item.description: Xavier Worthy has reportedly avoided charges from his arrest in Texas Friday night after Williamson County authorities spoke with witnesses.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Aggies dominate Air Force in regular season finale
item.description: LOGAN, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - Mason Falslev scored 18 points on 8-of-10 shooting to lead the Utah State basketball team to a dominating 87-47 win over Air Force. The Aggies had lost two in a row for the first time all year, but gave their fans plenty to cheer about in their home finale.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Ravens agree to a new contract with Pro Bowl LT Ronnie Stanley before free agency begins
item.description: Ronnie Stanley is returning to the Baltimore Ravens after the team announced a new deal with the veteran left tackle.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Angel Reese and Dijonai Carrington lash out at new WNBA fans over appearance comments
item.description: WNBA players Angel Reese and DiJonai Carrington revealed during a recent interview how comments about their appearance by fans bothers them.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Livvy Dunne dealing with injury that could end her gymnastics career
item.description: LSU gymnast Livvy Dunne, who has not competed since late January, said she's been dealing with a knee injury, leaving her future in doubt.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ejiofor's buzzer-beater in OT gives No. 6 St. John's an 86-84 win over No. 20 Marquette
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — Zuby Ejiofor hit a tiebreaking shot at the buzzer, Kadary Richmond got the first St. John’s triple-double this century and the sixth-ranked Red Storm beat No. 20 Marquette 86-84 in overtime Saturday for their sixth consecutive victory. St.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A diaper dandy? NASCAR Hall of Famer Tony Stewart ready to take steps in NHRA and fatherhood
item.description: GAINESVILLE, Fla. (AP) — New dad Tony Stewart insists he’s ready to change his first diaper. But it needs to happen "in the right situation.
item.dc:creator: MARK LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sellers and Rantanen are among the NHL trade deadline winners. Hurricanes and Boeser are some losers
item.description: Chris Patrick woke up Friday morning to see what trades around the NHL he missed. Quickly, the Washington Capitals' first-year general manager figured out that his colleagues around the league weren't bluffing about what they wanted in return.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nikola Jokic makes NBA history with 30-20-20 triple-double in Nuggets' OT win over Suns
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Nikola Jokic sat in front of his locker, calmly scrolling through his phone, seemingly unimpressed that 30 minutes earlier he made NBA history.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KELLY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Connor Bedard scores in overtime to lift Blackhawks to 4-3 win over Utah
item.description: Connor Bedard scored 3:10 into overtime to give the Chicago Blackhawks a 4-3 victory over the Utah Hockey Club on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A's announce 3-year Las Vegas sponsorship as club looks toward future move
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — The Athletics will play in Northern California for at least the next three seasons, but will also represent their future home in Las Vegas and brought four of their star players to drive home that point.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kenwood girls and boys basketball squads looking to make school history together
item.description: The Kenwood Girls Basketball team defeated Alton 61-55 Friday afternoon at the IHSA Class 4A semifinal to advance to the school’s first state championship game.
item.dc:creator: Josh Frydman, WGN 9

item.title: Reports: Bears trade for all-pro Chiefs OL Joe Thuney
item.description: LAKE FOREST, Ill. — The Bears have reportedly traded for four-time Super Bowl champion guard Joe Thuney from the Chiefs. According to multiple reports, the Bears are sending back a 2026 fourth-round pick for the 32-year-old guard.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, WGN 9

item.title: WNBA champion invests in Bay Area sports franchise
item.description: (KRON) – WNBA champion and Bay Area native Sabrina Ionescu was announced as an investor and commercial advisor for the Bay Football Club. Ionescu, who is also a three-time All-Star and Olympic gold medalist, announced her involvement with Bay FC on Instagram.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Baker, KRON4

item.title: Filipowski scores 25, but Jazz lose to Detroit, 134-106
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Cade Cunningham had 29 points, nine assists and four steals, and the Detroit Pistons beat the Utah Jazz 134-106 on Monday night for their 10th win in 11 games. The Jazz committed 27 turnovers end a season-long nine-gam homestand with a record of 3-6.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Luka scores 29 points and LeBron adds 17 in the Lakers' 2nd straight win over the Clippers, 108-102
item.description: Luka Doncic had 29 points and nine assists, LeBron James added 17 points and the Los Angeles Lakers hung on to beat the Los Angeles Clippers for the second time in three days, 108-102 on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Donato scores twice, Blackhawks snap 5-game skid with 6-3 road win over Ducks
item.description: ANAHEIM, Calif. (AP) — Ryan Donato had two goals and an assist, and the Blackhawks ended a five-game losing streak with a 6-3 road victory over the Anaheim Ducks on Saturday night. Teuvo Teravainen had a goal and two assists and Lukas Reichel, Wyatt Kaiser and Patrick Marron also scored.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
